common.name: Name
settings.title: Settings
settings.collapseSidebarDescription: Hide section labels and keep only icons
settings.themeDescription: Choose one of four application themes
settings.dataLocationDescription: BenchForge stores the database, artifacts, sandbox and preferences in the data folder next to the app/project — not AppData by default.
settings.updatesDescription: Check GitHub Releases and download a new ZIP/portable package without automatically replacing the running app.
settings.updatesNone: You are on the latest version.
settings.updatesNoRelease: No published GitHub Release exists for this repository yet. Create a release and upload artifacts first.
settings.updatesInstallConfirm: BenchForge will download the ZIP, preserve the data folder, close the app, replace app files and start the new version. Continue?
settings.updatesInstallStarted: Update has started. BenchForge will close shortly.
settings.toolRuntimeDescription: Local tools for agent benchmarks: Python, Node, files and simple image tools.
settings.useDockerSandboxDescription: If Docker is available, Python/Node run with --network none in a container.
settings.judgeModelDescription: Model used to evaluate creative/manual answers.
settings.mcpServersDescription: Optional MCP servers launched through stdio. JSON config: [{"id":"fs","command":"npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem","C:/tmp"]}]
settings.mcpHelpBody:
1. Install/run an MCP server, for example via npx.
2. Paste JSON config with id, command and args.
3. Click “Save MCP”.
4. Click “Show MCP tools” to verify it works.
5. In a tool benchmark, add mcp.list_tools and mcp.call to allowed_tools.

Note: an MCP server has the permissions you give it in args, for example a filesystem directory.
settings.repoSandboxDescription: Map local repositories to repo/patch benchmarks. JSON: [{"repo":"django/django","path":"C:/src/django","testCommand":"python -m pytest tests/..."}]
settings.repoSandboxHelpBody:
1. Clone the repo locally, for example C:/src/django.
2. Add a JSON entry with repo or instanceId, path and optional testCommand.
3. Click “Save Repo Sandbox”.
4. Import SWE-bench Lite or a repo_patch benchmark.
5. The model returns a diff/patch, BenchForge copies the repo into artifacts, runs git apply and executes testCommand.

If testCommand is not configured, the maximum score for applying the patch is 50%.
settings.backupDatabaseDescription: Save one JSON file with models, benchmarks, tasks, results and run history
settings.restoreBackupDescription: Load a JSON backup file and overwrite the current database
settings.rebuildArtifactsDescription: Create prompt/response/thinking files for existing results without rerunning benchmarks
settings.clearAllDataDescription: Removes ALL models, benchmarks and results
settings.clearAllConfirm: Are you sure? This removes ALL models, benchmarks and results.
settings.importConfirm: Import will overwrite existing BenchForge data. Continue?
settings.importFailed: Could not import data. Check that the JSON file is valid.
settings.aboutDescription: Desktop app for benchmarking AI models.
settings.stack: Stack: Electron + React + TypeScript + Vite + Zustand + SQLite
onboarding.title: First-run setup

onboarding.welcomeBody: This short wizard helps you add a model, check tools and create your first test.

models.title: AI models
models.scanDescription: Scanning local endpoints and the provider selected in the form if the required API key was provided.
models.noScanned: No models found. Start the server for local providers, or provide API Key and correct Base URL for remote ones.
models.noMatchingScanned: No matching scanned models for this provider. Click “Scan models” or type manually.

models.providerIconHint: Shown as a small icon in the corner

benchmarks.title: Benchmarks
benchmarks.libraryDescription: Download known benchmarks and import them directly in BenchForge format.
benchmarks.libraryFallback: Used the built-in fallback because the remote source was unavailable: {error}
benchmarks.libraryNoPacks: No packs match the current filters.
benchmarks.templatesDescription: Create a ready benchmark without hand-writing JSON/pass_condition.

benchmarks.templateToolDescription: Agent benchmark requiring real local tool or MCP tool usage.
benchmarks.templateVisionDescription: Maze/path template with path.json and SVG overlay artifacts.
benchmarks.templateManualDescription: Qualitative benchmark for web/manual models and rubric scoring.
benchmarks.radarDescription: Discover community benchmarks from repositories containing benchforge.beacon and benchforge.json.
benchmarks.radarSecurity: Safe mode: BenchForge only downloads JSON from GitHub and never runs repository code automatically.
benchmarks.radarGithubTokenHint: Raises GitHub API limits. Stored locally in app preferences.
benchmarks.importArrayError: JSON must be an array of benchmarks
benchmarks.detailsDescription: Benchmark editing, task adding and deletion are in this popup.
benchmarks.taskListSubtitle: Click a benchmark rectangle to open the edit and tasks popup.
benchmarks.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, suite, category, description or tasks...
benchmarks.emptyDescription: Add a benchmark or import JSON.
benchmarks.noSearchResultsDescription: Change the search phrase or clear the filter.
benchmarks.codePresenceDescription: Mode: code must contain specific fragments.
benchmarks.toolConfigDescription: Agent mode: the model can call local tools and MCP within limits.
benchmarks.passConditionPlaceholder: Describe the condition or click 'Code must contain…' to add required code fragments.
radar.empty: No models or benchmarks for the radar.
radar.lowBenchmarkHint: You have fewer than 3 benchmarks, so the radar uses models as axes. Add 3+ benchmarks and the radar will switch to classic benchmark mode.
radar.fallbackHint: With 1–2 benchmarks, the radar chart cannot create a meaningful shape, so a fallback score preview is shown.
bars.empty: No models or benchmarks to display the chart.
trend.empty: No trend chart data — run a benchmark at least once.

manual.descriptionMaze: Paste the model answer containing path JSON.

manual.imageCopied: Copied! Paste it into the chatbot.
manual.mazeHelp: Copy the image, paste it into the chatbot with the prompt, then paste the JSON answer below.
manual.batchRepoWarning: A large SWE/repo batch may be truncated by the model or mix patches. You can continue — BenchForge will parse each task separately.
manual.batchCopyHint: Copy the prompt, paste it into the web model, then paste the answer below.
manual.batchPasteResponse: Model answer for the whole batch
manual.batchSomeFailed: Some batch answers were not saved.
manual.batchPromptIntro: Benchmark: {benchmark}. Answer {count} tasks. Keep a separate answer for each task_id.
manual.batchPromptFormat: Return ONLY blocks in the format below. Allowed task_id values: {taskIds}. Do not merge tasks.
manual.batchPromptScoreHint: If a task cannot be scored automatically, you may add score="0-100" or score="tak/nie" to the benchforge_result tag.
